front: What is DPP-IV and its role in clinical peptide pharmacology?
back: Dipeptidyl peptidase IV (CD26) is a serine protease that cleaves Xaa-Pro/Xaa-Ala dipeptides from N-terminus. Key substrates: GLP-1, GIP, SDF-1α. Clinical relevance: DPP-IV inhibitors (gliptins) extend incretin half-life for diabetes. Also: T-cell activation marker, HIV co-receptor.
